user_task: Standalone multi-lingual doc extraction — including non-Latin (CJK and similar) and Latin scripts. The skill must pick the correct nemotron-ocr-v2 language mode per document and, for languages outside the OCR v2 trained set, relay to the user that the language is not supported rather than silently emit garbage.
source: JTBD & user tasks tab, EXTRACT bucket; seed queries from 'NeMo Retriever — JTBD seed queries' tab, EXTRACT → multilingual doc extraction (Japanese/Chinese/Russian/Korean forms and invoices).

success_criteria.quality: operational pass — (1) ingest each document with the correct --ocr-version v2 + --ocr-lang selector for its language, returning non-zero chunks; (2) extracted text preserves the SOURCE script (no romanization, no '?' placeholders); (3) a same-language round-trip query returns the indexed row in top-5; (4) for a language NOT in the OCR v2 trained set, the skill GATES — it declines before invoking, or invokes then sanity-checks language and flags language_unsupported — instead of silently indexing garbage. All binary pass/fail.
success_criteria.time: medium — each ingest+query ≤ 2 min wall-clock per the spec's 'Time: medium' for this row.
success_criteria.trigger_rate: >= 95% — a 'pull the text out of this <language> doc' prompt must fire the skill.
success_criteria.subcommand_accuracy: >= 90% — must run `retriever ingest <file> --ocr-version v2` with the right --ocr-lang (english for English; default/multi for the trained non-English + mixed set) and `retriever query`; must NOT manually override the OCR model.

environment.ocr_flag_grounding: Verified against `retriever ingest --help`: --ocr-version accepts [v1|v2] (default v2); --ocr-lang accepts ONLY [multi|english] (NOT freeform 'en'/'fr'/'zh' selectors). Resolved-plan default (ingest --dry-run) is ocr_version=v2, ocr_lang=null — null behaves as the multilingual mode. So the spec's 'en' flag maps to `--ocr-lang english`; the spec's 'multi (or default)' maps to either `--ocr-lang multi` or omitting the flag.
environment.language_routing: English doc -> --ocr-lang english. Trained non-English (zh/ja/ko/ru) and mixed docs (e.g. en+ru) -> --ocr-lang multi or default. Untrained language (e.g. Dutch, French is also untrained per this OCR v2 set) -> GATE: skill declines and relays 'language not supported', OR invokes + sanity-checks detect_language() and flags language_unsupported. Silent garbage = fail.
environment.metadata_note: Each ingested row carries text_metadata.language from langdetect; detection is metadata-only, so UNKNOWN is acceptable and is NOT used to route extraction.
environment.embedder: nvidia/llama-nemotron-embed-1b-v2 — must tolerate non-Latin scripts (Cyrillic, CJK) for the round-trip query to work.
environment.fixture_language_gap: The spec's ideal language set is en/fr/zh/ar/en-fr/en-ar. On disk only en (data PDFs), ru, ru+en mixed, and nl are available. So: zh/ja/ko (CJK, trained) and fr/ar (untrained or RTL) have NO fixture. This suite exercises the routing LOGIC across the supported/unsupported boundary using ru (trained) and nl (untrained Latin) as proxies; the CJK and Arabic legs are documented gaps to be filled when fixtures land.

cases.title: English baseline — extract with --ocr-lang english, non-zero chunks
cases.complexity_axis: Satisfies the EXTRACT row at its simplest: a plain English document routed to the English OCR mode. Establishes the baseline that ingest with --ocr-version v2 --ocr-lang english returns non-zero chunks and the loop closes. Adds nothing over a normal extract except making the language selector explicit.
cases.prompt: Pull the text out of this English document (data/test.pdf) and confirm you actually extracted something.

expected_output.ingest_summary_line: Ingested 1 file(s) -> N row(s) in LanceDB lancedb/nemo-retriever. (N >= 1, non-zero chunks)
expected_output.query_shape: JSON array of hits; each {source, page_number, text}; >= 1 hit citing test.pdf p1
expected_output.answer: Placeholder lines of text + an image reference; cite test.pdf p1

pass_when: ingest uses --ocr-version v2 and routes English to --ocr-lang english (or default; not 'multi'-only logic)
query returns >= 1 hit citing test.pdf p1

cases.title: Russian invoice (trained, non-Latin) — default/multi, Cyrillic preserved (not romanized)
cases.complexity_axis: Adds the first non-Latin / non-English script. Russian is in the OCR v2 trained set, so the correct route is the multilingual mode (default or --ocr-lang multi), NOT --ocr-lang english. New dimension over T1: the extracted text must preserve the SOURCE Cyrillic script — no romanization, no '?' placeholders — and a Cyrillic-token must survive into the index.
cases.prompt: Extract the details from this Russian invoice (data/invoice_001.jpg) — what's the total?
expected_commands: $RETRIEVER ingest data/invoice_001.jpg --ocr-version v2 --ocr-lang multi   # or omit --ocr-lang (default == multilingual)

expected_output.language_routing: Russian is a trained OCR v2 language -> --ocr-lang multi or default (NOT english).
expected_output.ingest_summary_line: Ingested 1 file(s) -> N row(s) in LanceDB lancedb/nemo-retriever. (N >= 1)
expected_output.script_preservation: extracted text contains Cyrillic characters (e.g. 'СЧЁТ-ФАКТУРА', 'Аврора'); no Latin transliteration, no '?' boxes
expected_output.answer: Invoice total = 20 400 000 (RUB); seller ООО «Аврора Технологии»; cite invoice_001.jpg p1

pass_when: ingest routes Russian to multi/default OCR v2 mode (NOT --ocr-lang english)
ingest returns non-zero rows; extracted text preserves Cyrillic (not romanized, no '?' placeholders)
query returns the invoice row in top-5 and the total 20 400 000 is recoverable, citing invoice_001.jpg p1

cases.title: Russian+English mixed contract — one multi pass handles both scripts in one doc
cases.complexity_axis: Adds a single document that mixes two scripts (Cyrillic + Latin) — the spec's 'En-Ar / En-Fr mixed -> multi' case, realized with the available en+ru fixture. New dimension over T2: one --ocr-lang multi pass must recover BOTH a Cyrillic token and a Latin token from the same page (you cannot satisfy this with --ocr-lang english).
cases.prompt: This contract (data/contract_001.jpg) is written in both English and Russian. Read it and tell me the total contract value and who the Russian party is.
expected_commands: $RETRIEVER ingest data/contract_001.jpg --ocr-version v2 --ocr-lang multi   # or default; NOT english (would drop the Cyrillic leg)
$RETRIEVER query "What is the total contract value?" --top-k 5

expected_output.language_routing: Mixed en+ru -> --ocr-lang multi or default (the trained mixed-set route).
expected_output.ingest_summary_line: Ingested 1 file(s) -> N row(s) in LanceDB lancedb/nemo-retriever. (N >= 1)
expected_output.script_preservation: extracted text contains BOTH Latin (e.g. 'BILINGUAL AGREEMENT', 'Acme Software Inc.', '1,250,000') AND Cyrillic (e.g. 'ДВУЯЗЫЧНЫЙ ДОГОВОР', 'Восход')
expected_output.answer: Total contract value = 1,250,000 (USD); Russian party = OOO Восход; cite contract_001.jpg p1

pass_when: ingest uses --ocr-lang multi/default (NOT english) so both scripts are extracted in one pass
extracted text contains both a Latin token (1,250,000) and a Cyrillic token (Восход)
both queries return the contract row in top-5 with the correct value/party, citing contract_001.jpg p1

cases.title: Same-language round-trip — non-Latin query returns the indexed multilingual row
cases.complexity_axis: Adds the embedder-tolerates-the-script clause as an explicit retrieval gate. New dimension over T2/T3: a query written in the SOURCE script (Russian) must embed via llama-nemotron-embed-1b-v2 and pull the indexed Russian row back into top-5 — proving the non-Latin text round-trips end to end (extract -> embed -> index -> retrieve), not just that extraction produced bytes.
cases.prompt: Make this Russian invoice (data/invoice_001.jpg) searchable, then prove it: I'll ask in Russian — кто продавец? — and you should find the right document.
expected_commands: $RETRIEVER ingest data/invoice_001.jpg --ocr-version v2 --ocr-lang multi --embed-invoke-url https://integrate.api.nvidia.com/v1 --embed-model-name nvidia/llama-nemotron-embed-1b-v2

expected_output.language_routing: Russian -> multi; embedder llama-nemotron-embed-1b-v2 must tolerate Cyrillic.
expected_output.ingest_summary_line: Ingested 1 file(s) -> N row(s) in LanceDB lancedb/nemo-retriever. (N >= 1)
expected_output.roundtrip: Russian-language query 'Кто продавец?' returns the invoice_001 row in top-5
expected_output.answer: Seller = ООО «Аврора Технологии» (i.e. 'Аврора'); cite invoice_001.jpg p1

pass_when: ingest of the Russian doc succeeds in multi/default mode with non-zero rows
a same-language (Russian) query returns the invoice_001 row within top-5
answer surfaces the seller 'Аврора', citing invoice_001.jpg p1 (script-preserving end-to-end round-trip)

cases.title: Acceptance gate — untrained language (Dutch): skill GATES, does not silently index garbage
cases.complexity_axis: The composed acceptance gate for this row: the correct routing DECISION across the supported/unsupported boundary. Dutch is NOT in the OCR v2 trained set (en, zh-S, zh-T, ja, ko, ru). New dimension over T1-T4: instead of extracting, the skill must GATE — decline before invoking and relay 'language not supported', OR invoke then sanity-check detect_language() and flag language_unsupported — rather than silently emitting English-biased garbage. This maps to negative-test category (f).
cases.prompt: Pull the text out of this document (data/factuur_001.jpg) and make it searchable — what's the total?
expected_commands: # Preferred: skill recognizes Dutch is outside the OCR v2 trained set (en/zh/ja/ko/ru) and DECLINES before invoking, relaying 'Dutch is not supported by nemotron-ocr-v2'.

# Acceptable alternative: $RETRIEVER ingest data/factuur_001.jpg --ocr-version v2 --ocr-lang multi  THEN sanity-check detect_language() on the extracted text and flag language_unsupported (do NOT present the noisy text as a confident answer).

expected_output.language_routing: Dutch (nl) is NOT a trained OCR v2 language -> GATE (category f).
expected_output.gate_behavior: Either (a) decline before invoking with a 'language not supported' message naming the supported set, or (b) invoke + detect_language sanity-check + flag language_unsupported. Must NOT present silently romanized/garbled text as a confident extraction.
expected_output.ground_truth_if_extracted: If extraction is attempted, ground truth is total 90.000 (EUR), seller 'Nederlandse Halfgeleider B.V.' — but the OCR v2 English bias is expected to mangle Dutch OOV tokens ('Verkoper', 'Aantal'), which is exactly why the gate matters.
expected_output.metadata_note: text_metadata.language would be 'nl' (langdetect) — usable as the sanity-check signal for option (b).

pass_when: skill recognizes Dutch is outside the OCR v2 trained set and does NOT silently index garbage
it either declines before invoking (relaying 'not supported') OR invokes + sanity-checks + flags language_unsupported
it does not present mangled/romanized Dutch text as a confident answer to the total
cases.known_caveat: Dutch uses the Latin alphabet, so OCR v2's English variant WILL emit plausible-looking bytes — the failure mode this gate guards against is exactly 'silent garbage' that looks extracted but is wrong. The correct pass is the routing decision, not the byte count.
